1	"""Heartbeat + phase-2 concurrency probe for the compose off-loop fix.
2	
3	Runs the REAL run_s5_i3.main() compose path on a SMALL selection (2 sections,
4	cap-primary 1) with:
5	  (1) an event-loop heartbeat coroutine measuring MAX loop-gap (freeze detector),
6	  (2) a monkeypatch around the AUTHORITATIVE _compose_section_per_basket that
7	      counts CONCURRENT phase-2 (max overlap) + logs thread ident (proves off-loop).
8	
9	Mode is chosen by env HB_MODE:
10	  HB_MODE=offloop  -> real code (asyncio.to_thread wrap active)   [TREATMENT]
11	  HB_MODE=onloop   -> asyncio.to_thread patched to INLINE-sync    [CONTROL / pre-fix]
12	
13	Does NOT edit any production file.
14	"""
15	import asyncio
16	import os
17	import sys
18	import threading
19	import time
20	import types
21	
22	# --- probe state ---
23	_phase2_lock = threading.Lock()
24	_phase2_active = 0
25	_phase2_max = 0
26	_phase2_calls = 0
27	_phase2_threads = set()
28	_main_thread_ident = threading.get_ident()
29	
30	_hb_max_gap = 0.0
31	_hb_last = None
32	_hb_samples = 0
33	
34	
35	def _wrap_compose(orig):
36	    def _wrapped(*a, **k):
37	        global _phase2_active, _phase2_max, _phase2_calls
38	        with _phase2_lock:
39	            _phase2_active += 1
40	            _phase2_calls += 1
41	            _phase2_max = max(_phase2_max, _phase2_active)
42	            cur = _phase2_active
43	            _phase2_threads.add(threading.get_ident())
44	        off = threading.get_ident() != _main_thread_ident
45	        print(f"[PHASE2 ENTER] concurrent={cur} off_loop_thread={off} "
46	              f"tid={threading.get_ident()} t={time.monotonic():.2f}", flush=True)
47	        t0 = time.monotonic()
48	        try:
49	            return orig(*a, **k)
50	        finally:
51	            dt = time.monotonic() - t0
52	            with _phase2_lock:
53	                _phase2_active -= 1
54	                cur2 = _phase2_active
55	            print(f"[PHASE2 EXIT] dt={dt:.1f}s remaining_concurrent={cur2} "
56	                  f"t={time.monotonic():.2f}", flush=True)
57	    return _wrapped
58	
59	
60	async def _heartbeat():
61	    global _hb_max_gap, _hb_last, _hb_samples
62	    _hb_last = time.monotonic()
63	    while True:
64	        await asyncio.sleep(0.25)
65	        now = time.monotonic()
66	        gap = now - _hb_last
67	        _hb_last = now
68	        _hb_samples += 1
69	        if gap > _hb_max_gap:
70	            _hb_max_gap = gap
71	        if gap > 2.0:
72	            print(f"[HB FREEZE] loop_gap={gap:.1f}s t={now:.2f}", flush=True)

74

76	    hb = asyncio.create_task(_heartbeat())

162	def _strip_request_framing(text: str) -> str:
163	    """Fix 6: peel a leading greeting and/or a report-request lead-in ("please help me write a research
164	    report on ...") off a research question, leaving the TOPIC. Iterative (a greeting then a request),
165	    only strips the request lead when a real topic tail (>= 8 chars) remains, and fail-open returns the
166	    original normalized head if nothing usable survives."""
167	    original = " ".join(str(text or "").split())
168	    s = original
169	    if not s:
170	        return s
171	    prev = None
172	    while prev != s:
173	        prev = s
174	        s = _REQUEST_GREETING_RE.sub("", s, count=1).strip()
175	        m = _REPORT_REQUEST_RE.match(s)
176	        if m and (len(s) - m.end()) >= 8:
177	            s = s[m.end():].strip()
178	    if not s:
179	        return original
180	    if s[0].islower():
181	        s = s[0].upper() + s[1:]
182	    return s

185	def _report_title(question: str, cp4: dict) -> str:
186	    """P3-1: a clean H1 — a cp4-provided report title if present, else the research question's first
187	    sentence/clause word-safe truncated (never a raw mid-word 200-char slice of the whole question).
188	    Fix 6 (2026-07-10): request/instruction framing is stripped so the H1 is the topic, not the request."""
189	    pl = (cp4 or {}).get("payload", {}) or {}
190	    for key in ("report_title", "title"):
191	        t = str(pl.get(key) or (cp4 or {}).get(key) or "").strip()
192	        if t:
193	            return t
194	    q = " ".join(str(question or "").split())
195	    if not q:
196	        return "Research Report"
197	    q = _strip_request_framing(q)  # Fix 6: drop leading request/instruction framing
198	    m = re.search(r"[?.]", q)
199	    head = q[: m.end()] if (m and m.end() <= 220) else q
200	    if len(head) <= 200:
201	        return head
202	    cut = head[:200].rsplit(" ", 1)[0]
203	    return (cut.rstrip() + "…") if cut else head[:200]

206	def _section_body_for_render(title: str, verified_text: str, generic_gap_stubs: tuple) -> str:
207	    """Fix 3 + Fix 8 (2026-07-10 compose gear-loop): assemble one section's rendered body. Never emit a
208	    bare heading with zero body (Fix 3) — an EMPTY body renders an honest gap disclosure NAMING the
209	    section. Never repeat the identical generic production gap-stub paragraph verbatim across sections
210	    (Fix 8) — a generic stub is varied with the section title so each reads distinctly. Any real verified
211	    prose is returned unchanged."""
212	    body = (verified_text or "").strip()
213	    _t = " ".join(str(title or "").split()) or "this section"
214	    if not body:
215	        return (
216	            f'No verified claim was composed for this section ("{_t}"); it is a curator-actionable '
217	            f'gap. The retrieved sources for this section remain in the bibliography and verification '
218	            f'details for per-claim disposition.'
219	        )
220	    if body in (generic_gap_stubs or ()):
221	        return body.replace("this section", f'this section ("{_t}")', 1)
222	    return body

225	def _audit_section(idx, title, verified_text, shingles, ngram):
226	    """Line-by-line audit of one section's composed prose (§-1.1).
227	    Returns per-sentence records + counts. A 'sentence' is a heading-stripped prose unit.
228	
229	    P1-5 (2026-07-10): the quote-dump predicate now measures VERBATIM n-gram overlap between each
230	    emitted sentence and the fetched source spans (context-level) — a cited verbatim copy is STILL a
231	    quote-dump, so a ~95%-copy report can no longer read quote_dump=0. The old length+no-citation
232	    heuristic was blind to cited quote-dumps."""
233	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.block_page_chrome_scrub import is_block_page_chrome_sentence
234	    records = []
235	    n_sent = n_with_cite = n_chrome = n_quote_dump = 0
236	    for raw_line in (verified_text or "").split("\n"):
237	        line = raw_line.strip()
238	        if not line:
239	            continue
240	        if line.startswith("#") or line.startswith("|") or line.startswith("- ") and len(line) < 4:
241	            # heading / table row / bare bullet marker: not a prose claim sentence
242	            continue
243	        for sent in _SENT_SPLIT.split(line):
244	            s = sent.strip()
245	            if len(s) < 12:
246	                continue
247	            n_sent += 1
248	            has_ev = bool(_EV_TOKEN.search(s))
249	            has_num = bool(_NUM_CITE.search(s))
250	            has_cite = has_ev or has_num
251	            if has_cite:
252	                n_with_cite += 1
253	            is_chrome = False
254	            try:
255	                is_chrome = bool(is_block_page_chrome_sentence(s))
256	            except Exception:
257	                is_chrome = False
258	            if is_chrome:
259	                n_chrome += 1
260	            # P1-5 quote-dump = a verbatim n-content-word run copied from any fetched source span
261	            # (context-level; independent of whether the sentence carries a citation).
262	            is_quote_dump = _max_verbatim_run(_content_words(s), shingles, ngram)
263	            if is_quote_dump:
264	                n_quote_dump += 1
265	            if (not has_cite) or is_chrome or is_quote_dump:
266	                records.append({
267	                    "section": idx, "no_cite": not has_cite, "chrome": is_chrome,
268	                    "quote_dump": is_quote_dump, "text": s[:280],
269	                })
270	    return {
271	        "section_index": idx, "title": title,
272	        "sentences": n_sent, "with_citation": n_with_cite,
273	        "chrome_sentences": n_chrome, "quote_dump_sentences": n_quote_dump,
274	        "flagged": records,
275	    }

278	# Fix 5 (2026-07-10 compose gear-loop): a run-level marker tally. The compose modules emit their
279	# activation / writer-yield / judge markers at INFO; this handler counts them across the whole run and
280	# the counts are written into the cp5 payload (writer_forensics) so the NEXT read can verify activation
281	# BEHAVIOURALLY (was invisible: the driver never configured logging, so only WARNING+ reached the log).
282	class _MarkerTally(logging.Handler):
283	    _PATTERNS = {
284	        "synth_primary_fired": "[activation] synth_primary",
285	        "outline_echo_fired": "[activation] outline_echo",
286	        "uncovered_fact_withheld": "[activation] uncovered_fact_subject_gate",
287	        "prepass_complete": "pre-pass complete",
288	        "kspan_recovery_pass": "K-span recovery pass",
289	        "kspan_fallback": "-> K-span",
290	        "transport_reconnect": "fresh reconnect window",
291	        "transport_disconnect": "transport-disconnect",
292	        "judge_error": "judge error",
293	        "judge_empty_content": "empty or non-str judge content",
294	        "judge_total_deadline": "total_deadline_exceeded",
295	        "judge_retryable_fault": "judge retryable fault",
296	        "basket_all_chrome_skipped": "all SUPPORTS members screened as chrome",
297	    }
298	
299	    def __init__(self):
300	        super().__init__(level=logging.INFO)
301	        self.counts = {k: 0 for k in self._PATTERNS}
302	        self.prepass_lines = []
303	
304	    def emit(self, record):
305	        try:
306	            msg = record.getMessage()
307	        except Exception:
308	            return
309	        for key, needle in self._PATTERNS.items():
310	            if needle in msg:
311	                self.counts[key] += 1
312	        if "pre-pass complete" in msg:
313	            self.prepass_lines.append(msg[:300])

# ─────────────────────────── record / replay A/B shim ───────────────────────────
# Certifies kept/dropped verdict-set IDENTITY between a SERIAL control and the CONCURRENT
# treatment at ZERO LLM cost. One RECORD pass captures every NLI-judge (verdict,reason) and
# every writer draft keyed by a CONTENT hash; two REPLAY passes (control=serial, treatment=
# PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=2, real threads) return those SAME answers deterministically.
#
# The judge is a PURE function of (sentence, span) and the writer of (members, revise_reasons,
# group_mode, section_context) — so a content-keyed lookup returning the FIRST recorded value is
# reorder-invariant and thread-safe (no per-call position counter that could race). Consequently
# ANY difference between the serial and concurrent kept/dropped set is a GENUINE shared-state race
# in the compose/verify path the off-loop fix newly runs on worker threads — exactly the one risk
# structural transparency does not cover. Env: HB_REPLAY_MODE=record|replay, HB_REPLAY_FILE=<pkl>,
# HB_INLINE_TO_THREAD=1 (serial control), PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS.


def _writer_key(members, kw) -> str:
    parts = []
    for m in (members or []):
        parts.append(f"{getattr(m, 'evidence_id', '')}\x1f{getattr(m, 'direct_quote', '')}")
    rr = kw.get("revise_reasons") or []
    sc = kw.get("section_context") or {}
    payload = (
        "\x01".join(parts)
        + "\x02" + "\x01".join(str(x) for x in rr)
        + "\x02" + str(kw.get("group_mode"))
        + "\x02" + str(sorted((str(k), str(v)) for k, v in sc.items()))
    )
    return hashlib.sha256(payload.encode("utf-8", "surrogatepass")).hexdigest()


def _judge_key(sentence, span) -> str:
    return hashlib.sha256(
        (str(sentence) + "\x00" + str(span)).encode("utf-8", "surrogatepass")
    ).hexdigest()


_RR_LOCK = threading.Lock()
_RR_STATS = {"judge_calls": 0, "judge_miss": 0, "judge_divergent_keys": 0,
             "writer_calls": 0, "writer_miss": 0}


def _install_record_replay():

    store_path = os.environ["HB_REPLAY_FILE"]
    from src.polaris_graph.llm import entailment_judge as ej
    from src.polaris_graph.generator import abstractive_writer as aw

        orig_writer = aw._call_writer

            k = _judge_key(sentence, span)
            with _RR_LOCK:
                seq = judge_store.setdefault(k, [])
                if seq and seq[0] != tuple(r):
                    _RR_STATS["judge_divergent_keys"] += 1
                seq.append(tuple(r))
            return r

        async def _rec_writer(members, evidence_pool, **kw):
            d = await orig_writer(members, evidence_pool, **kw)
            k = _writer_key(members, kw)
            with _RR_LOCK:
                writer_store.setdefault(k, []).append(d)
            return d

        ej._EntailmentJudge.judge = _rec_judge
        aw._call_writer = _rec_writer

        import atexit

        def _dump():
            with open(store_path, "wb") as f:
                pickle.dump({"judge": judge_store, "writer": writer_store}, f)
            print(f"[RR record] dumped judge_keys={len(judge_store)} "
                  f"writer_keys={len(writer_store)} divergent_judge_keys="
                  f"{_RR_STATS['judge_divergent_keys']} -> {store_path}", flush=True)

        atexit.register(_dump)
        print(f"[RR record] installed (real LLM) -> {store_path}", flush=True)
    else:  # replay — return the FIRST recorded value per content key (deterministic, race-free)
        with open(store_path, "rb") as f:
            data = pickle.load(f)
        judge_store = data["judge"]
        writer_store = data["writer"]

        def _rep_judge(self, sentence, span):
            k = _judge_key(sentence, span)
            with _RR_LOCK:
                _RR_STATS["judge_calls"] += 1
                seq = judge_store.get(k)
                if not seq:
                    _RR_STATS["judge_miss"] += 1
            # deterministic fallback on an unseen content (should be 0 if replay==record inputs)
            return tuple(seq[0]) if seq else ("NEUTRAL", "replay_miss")

        async def _rep_writer(members, evidence_pool, **kw):
            k = _writer_key(members, kw)
            with _RR_LOCK:
                _RR_STATS["writer_calls"] += 1
                seq = writer_store.get(k)
                if not seq:
                    _RR_STATS["writer_miss"] += 1
            return seq[0] if seq else ""

        ej._EntailmentJudge.judge = _rep_judge
        aw._call_writer = _rep_writer
        print(f"[RR replay] installed (ZERO LLM) judge_keys={len(judge_store)} "
              f"writer_keys={len(writer_store)} from {store_path}", flush=True)


def _replay_main():
    """Record or replay run_s5_i3.main() with the LLM boundaries shimmed. In replay+control mode
    (HB_INLINE_TO_THREAD=1) asyncio.to_thread runs inline so compose is SERIAL on the loop."""
    import scripts.run_s5_i3 as runmod

    _install_record_replay()

    if os.environ.get("HB_INLINE_TO_THREAD", "0") == "1":
        async def _inline_to_thread(fn, *a, **k):
            return fn(*a, **k)
        asyncio.to_thread = _inline_to_thread
        print("[RR] asyncio.to_thread INLINED (serial control arm)", flush=True)

    t0 = time.monotonic()
    asyncio.run(runmod.main())
    print(f"[RR] run complete wall={time.monotonic()-t0:.1f}s stats={_RR_STATS}", flush=True)


def main():
    if os.environ.get("HB_REPLAY_MODE"):
        _replay_main()
        return
    mode = os.environ.get("HB_MODE", "offloop")

"""Compare two cp5 outputs (serial control vs concurrent treatment) for kept/dropped IDENTITY.

The kept sentence set of a section == its ``verified_text``; the dropped count == ``sentences_dropped``.
Byte-identical verified_text + identical verified/dropped counts across arms => the off-loop fix's
worker-thread concurrency changed NOTHING about the faithfulness verdict set. Exit 0 iff identical.
"""
import hashlib
import json
import sys


def _load(p):
    d = json.load(open(p, encoding="utf-8"))
    secs = {}
    for s in d["payload"]["section_drafts"]:
        secs[int(s["section_index"])] = {
            "verified_text": s.get("verified_text") or "",
            "sentences_verified": s.get("sentences_verified"),
            "sentences_dropped": s.get("sentences_dropped"),
            "is_gap_stub": s.get("is_gap_stub"),
        }
    return d, secs


def _sha(t):
    return hashlib.sha256(t.encode("utf-8", "surrogatepass")).hexdigest()[:16]


def main():
    ctrl_path, treat_path = sys.argv[1], sys.argv[2]
    ctrl_d, ctrl = _load(ctrl_path)
    treat_d, treat = _load(treat_path)

    all_idx = sorted(set(ctrl) | set(treat))
    ok = True
    print(f"{'sec':>3} {'ctrl_v':>6} {'ctrl_d':>6} {'treat_v':>7} {'treat_d':>7} "
          f"{'ctrl_sha':>16} {'treat_sha':>16}  match")
    for i in all_idx:
        c = ctrl.get(i, {})
        t = treat.get(i, {})
        c_sha = _sha(c.get("verified_text", ""))
        t_sha = _sha(t.get("verified_text", ""))
        text_match = c.get("verified_text") == t.get("verified_text")
        cnt_match = (c.get("sentences_verified") == t.get("sentences_verified")
                     and c.get("sentences_dropped") == t.get("sentences_dropped"))
        m = text_match and cnt_match
        ok = ok and m
        print(f"{i:>3} {str(c.get('sentences_verified')):>6} {str(c.get('sentences_dropped')):>6} "
              f"{str(t.get('sentences_verified')):>7} {str(t.get('sentences_dropped')):>7} "
              f"{c_sha:>16} {t_sha:>16}  {'OK' if m else 'DIFFER'}")

    # whole-report identity too
    rep_c = ctrl_d["payload"]["assembled_report_md"]
    rep_t = treat_d["payload"]["assembled_report_md"]
    rep_match = rep_c == rep_t
    print(f"\nassembled_report_md: ctrl_sha={_sha(rep_c)} treat_sha={_sha(rep_t)} "
          f"len_ctrl={len(rep_c)} len_treat={len(rep_t)} match={rep_match}")
    ok = ok and rep_match

    print(f"\n=== VERDICT-SET IDENTITY: {'IDENTICAL (PASS)' if ok else 'DIVERGED (FAIL)'} ===")
    sys.exit(0 if ok else 1)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
